Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Admissions And Reasonable Attorney'S Fees And Expenses; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Feb-28-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Ronald M. Toran for plaintiff and moving party: Maria Addiego and Rina Addiego
Lawrence Bennett for defendant and opposing party: California Pacific Medical Center

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted:
Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to requests for admissions is granted:
1, 2, 3, 4, 11
The objections are overruled and stricken. The answers are otherwise sufficient

5, 6, 7, 8
The objections are overruled and stricken. Defendant is ordered to respond within 15 days of notice of this order. The Court finds that the objections and opposition to the motion were made without substantial justification in light of the previous orders. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $636.30 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Prevailing party to submit form of order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Notice Of Moiton And Moiton To Compel Furthr Response To Inspection Demand And For Reasonable Atttorney'S Fees And Expenses
Off Calendar Jan-26-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Ronald Toran for plaintiff and moving party
Stanley M. Wessels for defendant and opposing party

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted with modification: Plaintiff's motion to compel further production of documents (set #6) is: denied in part and granted in part.
39
Denied. The request seeks information that is irrelevant to the subject matter of the action.
40
Granted in part. Defendant may redact the names and personal identifying information of any on-call physician who was not working on September 2, 2004 at the emergency department of the California Campus. For those on-call physicians who were working, Defendant may redact any personal identifying information.
41
Granted in part. Defendant is ordered to produce any EMTALA violation investigations that refer or relate to denials of or delays in providing medical services to persons requesting emergency medical care as a result of conditions, occurrences, or accidents on hospital property. "Hospital property" shall be defined pursuant to Policy No. 1.85 of defendant's administration manual (9/04 revision date). Any documents withheld pursuant to either the attorney client or attorney work product privilege shall be identified with particularity in a privilege log.
Defendant shall produce responsive documents, if any, on or before February 10, 2006. All requests for monetary sanctions are denied.
MODIFICATION: Compliance date: February 15, 2006. ORDER SIGNED. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Richard A. Canatella for plaintiff and moving party: Maria Addiego and Rina Addiego
Stanley M. Wessels for defendant and opposing party: California Pacific Medical Center

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to request for production of documents is granted. The objections are overruled and stricken. Defendant is ordered to produce responsive documents within 14 days of notice of this order. The Court finds that defendant made an evasive response to discovery in that plaintiff properly requested information identified in defendant's response to form interrogatory 4.1. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $636.30 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. ORDER SIGNED. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
